mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159069 thousand as of Jan 2013 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.5% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that compresses equity multiples. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates rise and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.086 as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic contraction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1499.0 thousand units as of Dec 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle demand weakness or early recession signal
failure_condition: Negative HOUST shocks produce negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains stable below 4.5%, indicating supply-side expansion without wage-driven inflation. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand from productivity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.511 and UNRATE at 4.0% as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with labor market slack
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in > 45% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling consumer demand weakness and forward earnings pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reduce growth forecasts.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 723853.0 million dollars as of Jan 2013
failure_condition: Consecutive weak RSAFS months produce negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and supports valuation expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when rate cuts are sustained.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or crisis response
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or sustained rate declines fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month while payroll growth (PAYEMS) turns negative, signaling rapid labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice recession risk sharply upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% and PAYEMS at 159069 thousand as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: early recession or sharp labor market shock
failure_condition: Joint labor shocks produce negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1.5 million units annualized, indicating broad-based demand expansion. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in late-cycle expansion regimes as corporate earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST > 1.5 million units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23530.909 billion chained 2017 dollars and HOUST at 1499.0 thousand as of Q4 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in > 45% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
